details: The agent found 75 labels but the known fact states there are approximately 124 labels. The agent's approach of sampling issues and PRs to discover labels was inherently incomplete - it missed roughly 49 labels (~39% of the total). The agent acknowledged this limitation but stopped searching prematurely without exhausting systematic approaches to find all labels. The significant undercount (75 vs ~124) means the answer is neither complete nor accurate enough to pass.
